articles.title: Game-Day energy goes global with Delta’s added Europe flights
articles.summary: Delta Air Lines is adding four special round-trip flights between the U.S. and Europe in November to accommodate fans traveling to major international professional football games in Madrid and Munich. These flights will depart from Atlanta and Detroit and will be operated on Delta's Airbus A330 aircraft, offering a range of premium experiences. The additional service highlights Delta's commitment to its trans-Atlantic network and providing enhanced travel options for customers.
articles.content: Delta Air Lines is adding four special round-trip flights between the U.S. and Europe in November to accommodate fans traveling to major international professional football games in Madrid and Munich. These flights will depart from Atlanta and Detroit and will be operated on Delta's Airbus A330 aircraft, offering a range of premium experiences. The additional service highlights Delta's commitment to its trans-Atlantic network and providing enhanced travel options for customers.

articles.title: Only 52% Full: Delta Air Lines' 10 Emptiest International Routes [Updated]
articles.summary: Delta Air Lines, the second-largest US operator for international passengers, filled 84.8% of its international seats in the 12 months to January 2026. This article identifies Delta's 10 international routes with the lowest load factors during that period, with the Atlanta to St. Vincent route being the "worst performing" at 48.2% due to its recent launch. Other routes with low load factors include JFK to Lagos, JFK to Kingston, and Miami to Havana, with some experiencing reduced frequencies or being seasonal to improve performance.
articles.content: Delta Air Lines, the second-largest US operator for international passengers, filled 84.8% of its international seats in the 12 months to January 2026. This article identifies Delta's 10 international routes with the lowest load factors during that period, with the Atlanta to St. Vincent route being the "worst performing" at 48.2% due to its recent launch. Other routes with low load factors include JFK to Lagos, JFK to Kingston, and Miami to Havana, with some experiencing reduced frequencies or being seasonal to improve performance.

articles.title: Geothermal firm boosted by Devon
articles.summary: Geothermal power developer Fervo Energy recently debuted on Nasdaq with an initial public offering of $27 per share, raising $1.89 billion and achieving a $7.7 billion valuation. Oklahoma City's Devon Energy is a significant shareholder, having made a strategic $10 million investment three years prior and assisting in further funding rounds. Other major investors include Capricorn Investment Group and Google, highlighting strong investor interest in clean energy, especially amidst the AI boom.
articles.content: Geothermal power developer Fervo Energy recently debuted on Nasdaq with an initial public offering of $27 per share, raising $1.89 billion and achieving a $7.7 billion valuation. Oklahoma City's Devon Energy is a significant shareholder, having made a strategic $10 million investment three years prior and assisting in further funding rounds. Other major investors include Capricorn Investment Group and Google, highlighting strong investor interest in clean energy, especially amidst the AI boom.

articles.title: Teradyne Inc. stock (US8807701029): French fund buys shares amid strong Q1 growth
articles.summary: French asset manager AG2R LA Mondiale Gestion d'Actifs recently acquired shares in Teradyne Inc., a semiconductor test equipment provider, following the company's strong financial performance, which included an 87% year-over-year revenue increase. Teradyne also held its annual shareholders meeting, with a director receiving restricted stock units, and showcased robust metrics like a 31.79% return on equity and 22.55% net margin. The company's core business in automated test systems and robotics positions it as a key player in the growing semiconductor industry, benefitting from demand for AI c
articles.content: French asset manager AG2R LA Mondiale Gestion d'Actifs recently acquired shares in Teradyne Inc., a semiconductor test equipment provider, following the company's strong financial performance, which included an 87% year-over-year revenue increase. Teradyne also held its annual shareholders meeting, with a director receiving restricted stock units, and showcased robust metrics like a 31.79% return on equity and 22.55% net margin. The company's core business in automated test systems and robotics positions it as a key player in the growing semiconductor industry, benefitting from demand for AI chips and 5G technology.

articles.title: Entergy Corp. stock (US29364G1031): JPMorgan raises price target to $129
articles.summary: JPMorgan Chase has increased its price target for Entergy Corp. (NYSE: ETR) to $129 from $126, while maintaining an overweight rating, reflecting confidence in the utility's future. This adjustment implies a potential 14% upside from current levels, fueled by recent equity offerings and shareholder approvals. Entergy, a major U.S. utility, continues to advance its capital plans across key southern markets and focuses on grid modernization and clean energy transitions.
articles.content: JPMorgan Chase has increased its price target for Entergy Corp. (NYSE: ETR) to $129 from $126, while maintaining an overweight rating, reflecting confidence in the utility's future. This adjustment implies a potential 14% upside from current levels, fueled by recent equity offerings and shareholder approvals. Entergy, a major U.S. utility, continues to advance its capital plans across key southern markets and focuses on grid modernization and clean energy transitions.

articles.title: NextEra Inks $9.5 Million Deal to Settle Wage Price-Fixing Case
articles.summary: NextEra Energy Inc. has agreed to a $9.5 million settlement to resolve antitrust claims alleging it conspired with other nuclear power generation companies to illegally suppress employee compensation. The proposed class action lawsuit, filed last July, accused the companies of exchanging information on worker pay and discussing ways to limit compensation. A notice of the settlement was filed in the US District Court for the District of Maryland, with plaintiffs expected to seek preliminary approval soon.
articles.content: NextEra Energy Inc. has agreed to a $9.5 million settlement to resolve antitrust claims alleging it conspired with other nuclear power generation companies to illegally suppress employee compensation. The proposed class action lawsuit, filed last July, accused the companies of exchanging information on worker pay and discussing ways to limit compensation. A notice of the settlement was filed in the US District Court for the District of Maryland, with plaintiffs expected to seek preliminary approval soon.

articles.title: CenterPoint Energy stock (US15189T1079): Jefferies and JPMorgan adjust price targets
articles.summary: Jefferies and JPMorgan have adjusted their price targets for CenterPoint Energy (US15189T1079) stock. Jefferies cut its target to $48 from $49, maintaining a Buy rating, while JPMorgan reduced its target to $45 from $46, holding a Neutral rating. Despite these adjustments, CenterPoint Energy's stock has seen a 9.9% year-to-date increase, trading at $42.15, highlighting continued investor interest in its regulated utility model and exposure to high-growth regions like Texas.

articles.title: Ford’s stock is the S&P 500’s biggest gainer. The carmaker is putting a very Tesla spin on things.
articles.summary: Ford’s stock is having its best day in six years, after Morgan Stanley gave a rosy outlook for the automaker’s energy business.
